title: Shipwright Triage JSONL Wire Format
description: Codifies the line-level wire shape of `<project>/.shipwright/triage.jsonl` — the pre-backlog triage inbox. Three event kinds share the file: a one-time header (line 1), append events (one per new triage item), and status events (one per Promote/Dismiss/Snooze). Producers go through `shared/scripts/triage.py::append_triage_item` or `::append_triage_item_idempotent`; consumers (`triage_cli.py`, `aggregate_triage.py`, WebUI Triage tab) read via `read_all_items` which collapses status events into the latest per id.

header.description: First line of every triage.jsonl. Bootstrapped by `_ensure_header` on the first append into a fresh project.

v.description: Schema version (currently 1). Bump on breaking wire-format changes.

append.description: A new triage item. Emitted by a producer when something needs operator attention.

originalTs.description: Authoring timestamp — preserved on idempotent re-append so dedup-window logic operates against the first sighting.

source.description: Free-form producer identifier. Open vocabulary (no enum) so new producers don't need a schema bump. Conventional values include: phase-quality, compliance, security, performance, surface-verify, drift, github, github-ci, github-secrets, github-security, sbom, test-evidence.

severity.description: Triage priority — drives suggestedPriority + default CTA. See `triage.SEVERITIES`.

kind.description: Coarse category — drives suggested backlog domain on Promote. See `triage.KINDS`.

evidencePath.description: Optional path (relative to project root) to scanner output, log file, or report backing this item.

runId.description: Optional iterate / pipeline run id where the producer ran. Lets consumers correlate cross-artifact.

commit.description: Optional commit SHA the producer observed.

dedupKey.description: Producer-supplied stable identifier for the underlying issue. Idempotent producers MUST supply one; freeform producers MAY. Convention: `<producer>:<key>` (e.g. `gh-security:owner/repo`, `phase-quality:C1`, `sbom:undeclared:server`).

launchPayload.description: Optional ready-to-paste block (typically a slash command + GitHub URL) shown by the inbox view's Fix-now CTA. Frozen at first append; idempotent re-emit leaves the on-disk value unchanged.

frId.description: Optional functional-requirement id this item is rooted in (e.g. `FR-01.05`). Iterate B0 (2026-05-21) — lets the RTM renderer emit a clickable `FAIL → trg-XXX` link from the matching row directly into `triage_inbox.md`.

suiteId.description: Optional test-suite identifier (e.g. `client/src/auth.test.tsx`, `pytest::shared.tests.test_x`). Same B0 use as `frId` — RTM / test-evidence cross-link.

eventId.description: Optional `shipwright_events.jsonl` event id this triage item corresponds to. Lets compliance reports back-reference the originating event.

status.description: Initial status — `triage` on every fresh append. Subsequent status events flip this via mark_status.

suggestedPriority.description: Derived from severity by `suggest_priority_from_severity` — critical→P0, high→P1, medium→P2, low/info→P3.

suggestedDomain.description: Derived from source by `suggest_domain_from_source` (only `compliance` is special-cased today).

status.description: A status flip — Promote / Dismiss / Snooze emitted by `triage.mark_status` (via `triage_cli.py`, WebUI, or `triage_promote.py`).

reason.description: Optional reason. Convention values used today: `notRelevant`, `flaky`, `alreadyFixed`, `schemaMigration`, `stale`.

promotedTaskId.description: Set when newStatus=`promoted` — references the created ExternalTask (e.g. `EXT:foo-001`).

_isoZ.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp with `Z` suffix — see `triage._now_z`.
